# Source Card: Diagnosis and management of acquired aplastic anemia in childhood (AIEOP Marrow Failure Study Group, 2024)

## Summary
This is a 2024 AIEOP (Italian Association of Pediatric Hemato-Oncology) Marrow Failure Study Group consensus practice guideline on the diagnosis and management of acquired aplastic anemia (AA) in children. For the pediatric CDS module it establishes the disease construct (pancytopenia plus hypoplastic bone marrow), an epidemiologic incidence rate, and a first-/second-line treatment hierarchy for severe aplastic anemia (SAA). The full text is paywalled; all asserted points are drawn from the verbatim published abstract and PubMed metadata, and the numeric disease-severity (Camitta-type) classification thresholds could NOT be located in accessible text and are recorded as a gap.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) Incidence 2-3 per million/year in the Western world, ~3x higher in East Asia — locator: Abstract sentence 2 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:112abc9827a91caa99e28edf06b3374a1e8ffd389e522325ea588f018729f268]"
- (ev_002) AA = pancytopenia + hypoplastic bone marrow — locator: Abstract sentence 1 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:aa89571e73682dcc6d5649cae3de6a4db5cc77c72a5eabe33c6a83da5f14db76]"
- (ev_003) SAA first-line = matched-sibling-donor HSCT — locator: Abstract, treatment sentence — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:d6d7339c6d4f6d823f76d8792ed12a31a624e98c8daf8e6ce3dde7c68f31a820]"
- (ev_004) No MSD → IST / MUD / haploidentical HSCT — locator: Abstract, treatment sentence — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:58e0a699b284a4254d60a0b8b29cb7457106d9fb1ef2efa166a47fb033f16453]"
- (ev_005) AIEOP consensus practice guideline provenance — locator: Abstract closing sentence + PubMed article-type (Practice Guideline) — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:aaa455cc4cc35b0524091f9d67e10a727693f5ac73e6bdb3f98b0e487e74700b]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: children (pediatric) with acquired aplastic anemia; abstract does not stratify by age band, sex, or gestational/postnatal age. The ev_001 incidence rate is a general-population geographic figure (Western world vs East Asia), not a pediatric-only or age-banded rate.
- Assay/method: diagnosis rests on peripheral-blood cell counts (pancytopenia) plus bone-marrow cellularity/histology; specific analyzer/method dependence is not stated in the accessible text.
- Lifecycle: guideline version = AIEOP Marrow Failure Study Group consensus 2024; published 2024-05 (Blood Cells Mol Dis vol. 108, art. 102860); no retirement/supersession date stated.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- Implicitly, a diagnosis of aplastic anemia (pancytopenia + hypoplastic marrow) is itself a specialist-referral condition requiring bone-marrow biopsy and pediatric hemato-oncology management; however, this source does not define an explicit, quantified red-flag/referral CDS trigger in the accessible text. Severity-based triage thresholds are the relevant scope-boundary data but are not extractable here (see Limitations).

## Limitations & conflicts
- GAP (numeric): The disease-severity classification thresholds for Severe Aplastic Anemia (SAA), Very Severe Aplastic Anemia (VSAA), and non-severe/moderate AA — i.e., the Camitta/Marsh-type neutrophil, platelet, and reticulocyte count cutoffs and the bone-marrow cellularity percentage — are NOT locatable in accessible text of this source (full text paywalled; not in PMC/Europe PMC; isOpenAccess=N). Per contract, these numbers are therefore NOT asserted here. They must be captured verbatim from the full text (or a licensed copy) before any CDS threshold rule is proposed.
- Recency/scope: national (Italian, AIEOP) consensus; may differ from BSH/EBMT/North American (NAPAAC, Shimano 2024) pediatric SAA recommendations. No conflict is asserted because the source-specific criteria could not be read; any cross-guideline threshold conflict must be preserved (not averaged) when the full-text numbers are later obtained.
- ev_003/ev_004 are captured as guideline content (source-supported facts about what the guideline states), not as executable treatment directives; rf output is a proposal for the CDS converter, not an actionable order.

Attribution: According to PubMed, this article is indexed at PMID 38889660, DOI [10.1016/j.bcmd.2024.102860](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.bcmd.2024.102860).
